---
title: Foundations
description: "The concepts every decision rests on: measurement scales, descriptives, normality, outliers, and the logic of hypothesis testing"
---

Before choosing a test, you need to know four things about your data: what **scale** each variable lives on, what its **distribution** looks like, whether it has **outliers**, and what **hypothesis** your design is built to test. This section covers those prerequisites.

## Pages in this section

- [Measurement scales](/decision-tree/foundations/measurement-scales.qmd) -- nominal, ordinal, interval, ratio, and what each permits.
- [Descriptive univariate statistics](/decision-tree/foundations/descriptive-univariate.qmd) -- mean, median, mode, and dispersion measures.
- [Normality checks](/decision-tree/foundations/normality.qmd) -- Shapiro-Wilk, Kolmogorov-Smirnov, and graphical diagnostics.
- [Outliers](/decision-tree/foundations/outliers.qmd) -- detection with boxplots, IQR fences, Grubbs, and Mahalanobis distance.
- [Hypotheses, significance, power](/decision-tree/foundations/hypotheses-significance.qmd) -- H0 / H1, alpha / beta, effect size, confidence intervals.

Every subsequent method page assumes fluency with these ideas, so a first-time reader should work through them in order.

----

*Structure inspired by the University of Zurich Methodenberatung (methodenberatung.uzh.ch). All text, examples, R code, and reporting sentences are independently authored in English.*
